channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Are Argentina being treated favourably at World Cup?
item.description: Egypt have alleged bias in favour of Argentina and favouritism towards their star player, Lionel Messi. Is this fair?

item.title: England players granted day off after Mexico win
item.description: England manager Thomas Tuchel gave his players permission for an offsite family day on Tuesday as the squad recharged after their euphoric win over Mexico.

item.title: The key players walking disciplinary tightrope
item.description: Four England players are at risk of missing a potential World Cup semi-final because of the tournament's yellow-card rules.

item.title: London City sign Ballon d'Or winner Putellas
item.description: London City Lionesses sign two-time Ballon d'Or winner Alexia Putellas on a three-year deal in one of the biggest scoops in women's football history.

item.title: A Golden Boot race for the ages - but who will come out on top?
item.description: It's fast becoming a World Cup Golden Boot for the ages. But who will win it?

item.title: Egypt want officials kicked out of World Cup
item.description: The Egyptian FA lodges a complaint with Fifa and demands an investigation into "double standards" of officiating in the controversial defeat by Argentina.

item.title: Henderson has surgery in USA after breaking arm
item.description: Jordan Henderson has had surgery after breaking his arm during the celebrations of England's win over Mexico on Sunday.

item.title: Amorim apologises for silence towards Man Utd fans
item.description: Ruben Amorim apologises for not speaking to Manchester United's fans since his dismissal in January but says he was "proud" to have been their coach.

item.title: Portugal confirm departure of coach Martinez
item.description: The Portuguese football federation (FPF) confirm the departure of national team coach Roberto Martinez following Portugal's last-16 loss to Spain.

item.title: 'Your boys took a hell of a beating' - England and Norway's football rivalry
item.description: England's meeting with Norway in the World Cup quarter-final revives memories of a commentary more famous than their rivalry.

item.title: Meet Sweden's top scorer who hopes to play for Scotland
item.description: Robbie Ure is thriving with Allsvenskan leaders IK Sirius, but Scotland may have competition from Ukraine for the in-form striker.

item.title: Watch every penalty from Switzerland v Colombia shootout
item.description: Watch the full penalties as Switzerland beat Colombia 4-3 in the shootout to advance to the quarter-finals.

item.title: Argentina make incredible late comeback to beat Egypt
item.description: Argentina survive an almighty scare before delivering a scarcely believable second-half comeback to break Egypt hearts and reach the quarter-finals of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Switzerland beat Colombia on penalties to reach quarter-finals
item.description: Gregor Kobel is Switzerland's penalty hero, after pulling off a stunning shootout save from Colombia's Cucho Hernandez to help set up a quarter-final clash with Argentina.

item.title: VAR controversially rules out Egypt goal
item.description: Mostafa Zico has his goal for Egypt ruled out following a VAR review, for a foul on Lisandro Martinez in the build up, during their World Cup last 16 match in Atlanta.

item.title: Fernandez header seals dramatic Argentina comeback
item.description: Enzo Fernandez scores late to give Argentina the lead against Egypt in their last 16 match at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Messi rescues Argentina with equaliser against Egypt
item.description: Lionel Messi scores a late equaliser for Argentina against Egypt to level the game at 2-2 during their match in Atlanta.

item.title: Why Newcastle target Manzambi is in demand
item.description: Johan Manzambi has been one of the World Cup's breakout stars, so can Newcastle United win the race to sign the Freiburg midfielder?

item.title: Has World Cup signalled end of chaos at corners?
item.description: Can the Premier League learn anything from the refereeing of corners at the World Cup?

item.title: The World Cup stars who could have played for England
item.description: While there is no shortage of talent in the England squad, a number of World Cup stars from other nations could have featured for the Three Lions if things had worked out differently.

item.title: A striker duel for the ages & Qatar revenge - what to expect in quarter-finals
item.description: Setting the scene for each of the four intriguing World Cup quarter-finals that begin on Thursday.

item.title: World Cup quiz: Teams to have played the most quarter-finals
item.description: With the first World Cup quarter-final taking place tomorrow we've been inspired to make this quiz.

item.title: Dalic leaves as Croatia coach after nine years
item.description: Croatia head coach Zlatko Dalic steps down after nine years in charge following their defeat against Portugal in the World Cup.

item.title: 'Gutted' Christie 'desperate' to help Scotland to more finals
item.description: At 31, Bournemouth midfielder Ryan Christie is determined to help Scotland reach more major finals after his World Cup disappointment.

item.title: Unusual or predictable? How Europe is dominating the World Cup
item.description: Six of the eight quarter-finalists at the World Cup in North America are from Europe, so why are they doing so well and what does history tell us about the chances of one of them winning it all?

item.title: Meet the duo visiting all 16 World Cup venues in a London cab
item.description: Meet Ollie Jenks and Seth Scott, who are attempting to visit all 16 World Cup stadiums in a traditional London taxi.

item.title: Wrexham to 'hold nerve' in transfer window - Parkinson
item.description: Wrexham manager Phil Parkinson urging patience after a slow start to the transfer window by the Championship club.

item.title: Rogers is Arsenal's main target - Wednesday's gossip
item.description: Arsenal make Morgan Rogers their main target, Liverpool interested in Mexico teenager and Everton eye England defender Spence.

item.title: Goals galore - how dominant is Premier League wealth at World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport explores just how dominant the Premier League's wealth is at the World Cup.

item.title: How to take a World Cup shootout penalty
item.description: We have a look at what can be learned from every World Cup penalty taken in a shootout ever.

item.title: Fulham appoint Arbeloa as Silva's successor
item.description: Fulham appoint Alvaro Arbeloa as their new manager on a three-year contract, succeeding Marco Silva who left for Benfica.

item.title: Messi denied from penalty spot by 'brilliant' Shobeir save
item.description: Lionel Messi misses his second penalty of the 2026 World Cup, as his effort is saved by Egypt's Mostafa Shobeir in Atlanta.

item.title: Belgium make light work of US to set up quarter-final tie with Spain
item.description: Belgium ease past a defensively sloppy USA in a match shrouded in controversy surrounding Folarin Balogun's delayed suspension, a scandal that made little impact on the pitch.

item.title: 'Total calamity!' US gift Belgium a third goal
item.description: USA goalkeeper Matt Freese with a huge error in judgement as he gifts Belgium's Hans Vanaken the ball, leaving him with an easy finish that defender Tim Ream could've done more to stop. Errors all around as Belgium take control of the round of 16 tie.

item.title: Spain into quarter-finals after Merino's late winner against Portugal
item.description: Spain leave it late as super-sub Mikel Merino scores a stoppage-time winner to send his side into the quarter-finals, ending Cristiano Ronaldo's hopes of World Cup glory.

item.title: Super-sub Merino delivers 'killer goal' for Spain
item.description: Spain midfielder Mikel Merino coolly scores in added time to break Portuguese hearts, sending his side through to the quarter-finals of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: How England's epic night at the Azteca unfolded
item.description: Relive all the drama from England’s 3-2 win over Mexico, as Thomas Tuchel’s team progressed to a quarter-final meeting with Norway on Saturday.

item.title: England win epic tie against Mexico to reach quarter-finals
item.description: Jude Bellingham scores twice and Harry Kane converts a penalty as 10-man England beat Mexico 3-2 in the World Cup.

item.title: Bellingham scores two in 98 seconds to silence the Azteca
item.description: Jude Bellingham scores two goals in 98 seconds to give England a 2-0 lead over Mexico during their last 16 match at the Mexico City Stadium.

item.title: Tears of relief? Messi adds another incredible chapter to World Cup career
item.description: Argentina great Lionel Messi sheds tears as they fight back to beat Egypt and reach the World Cup quarter-finals.

item.title: Blackburn Rovers sued for £500k by kit suppliers
item.description: The Lancashire club is facing the proceedings after negotiations for a kit renewal deal broke down.

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 31
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: England on course to face Argentina in semis? See their path to final
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look at what England will need to do to reach the World Cup final after progressing to the quarter-finals.

item.title: Shades of Keane and Gerrard in Bellingham display - Rooney
item.description: Wayne Rooney believes Jude Bellingham's performance against Mexico ranks alongside iconic Steven Gerrard and Roy Keane displays

item.title: The superstar double act that became England's Wonderwall
item.description: With 10 of England's 11 goals at the World Cup scored by either Harry Kane or Jude Bellingham, BBC Sport looks at how dependent on them the team is.

item.title: Arsenal sign Switzerland midfielder Reuteler
item.description: Arsenal sign Switzerland midfielder Geraldine Reuteler following her exit from Eintracht Frankfurt.

item.title: Villa and Belgium midfielder Onana ruptures ACL
item.description: Aston Villa midfielder Amadou Onana ruptures an anterior cruciate ligament while playing for Belgium at the World Cup.

item.title: La Liga chief critical of 'complicit silence' surrounding Fifa
item.description: La Liga president Javier Tebas has criticised the "complicit silence" which surrounds Fifa after the Folarin Balogun controversy.

item.title: Can Scotland learn from history-making Norway?
item.description: Norway, a nation of a similar size to Scotland, are through to the World Cup quarter-finals, so can lessons be learned from their neighbours from across the North Sea?

item.title: Fifa investigating alleged racist abuse of IShowSpeed
item.description: Fifa has opened an investigation following allegations of racist abuse involving American social media influencer IShowSpeed and a supporter during Argentina's 3-2 win over Cape Verde on Friday.

item.title: Fifa's Balogun decision could lead to football 'cesspit' - McAllister
item.description: Uefa vice-president Laura McAllister says there is a risk of "an absolute cesspit" of political interference in football after USA striker Folarin Balogun avoided a ban at the World Cup.

item.title: England players to get hoarding warning after freak Henderson injury
item.description: England players will be advised not to jump over advertising hoardings after Jordan Henderson suffered a freak injury.

item.title: A shambolic end for the American dream - did Balogun saga play part?
item.description: Mauricio Pochettino says he feels "disappointed with too many people" who brought "politics and manipulation" into the game as the US exit World Cup.

item.title: Why European backlash over Trump intervention won't worry Infantino
item.description: After 10 years as Fifa president, could the Folarin Balogun controversy tip the balance against Gianni Infantino? Don't bet on it.

item.title: How England used five mini games to beat Mexico
item.description: The tactical breakdown of how Thomas Tuchel's 10-man England pulled off a remarkable World Cup win in a hostile Mexico City.

item.title: An incredible night when England heroes were made
item.description: On a spine-tingling night of drama, emotion and pure theatre in one of sport's most atmospheric arenas, England delivered one of their greatest performances.

item.title: Murals, flags and beef dishes - Messi mania takes hold in Miami
item.description: Lionel Messi's World Cup goals roadshow continued with his seventh of the tournament for Argentina against Cape Verde - and delighted the fans who were in Miami to see their hero.

item.title: Farewell Cape Verde - the underdogs the World Cup will never forget
item.description: Cape Verde may have been the World Cup's second-smallest nation but they leave the largest impact as they depart following defeat by defending champions Argentina in an epic.

item.title: All done deals in June 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Should teams bring on players just for penalty shootouts?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at the numbers behind late substitutes taking penalties in shootouts.

item.title: Who has been eliminated from the World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team lists the full set of nations who have been eliminated from the 2026 World Cup so far

item.title: How are the TV picks decided for the World Cup's round of 32?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the complexities behind television selections for the 2026 World Cup's round of 32 fixtures

item.title: Is five-match ban for Qatar's Madibo excessive?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at whether Madibo's punishment for his challenge on Kone was proportionate.

item.title: What is the World Cup prize money?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team break down the prize money on offer at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Who is the African World Cup goalscorer older than Ronaldo and Messi?
item.description: While Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi appear to be in a personal battle to rewrite the World Cup record books, there is one landmark that appears beyond them.

item.title: Why Saudi Arabia and Iraq flags are not laid down at World Cup
item.description: The flags of Saudi Arabia and Iraq are not laid on the ground during World Cup pre-match ceremonies because they include religious wording.

item.title: What are World Cup's red card suspension rules?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains Fifa's suspension rules on red and yellow cards during the World Cup.

item.title: Qarabag's Duran poised for Celtic medical
item.description: Colombian forward Camilo Duran is poised to become Celtic's first summer signing, arriving for an undisclosed fee from Qarabag, manager Martin O'Neill confirms.

item.title: Benitez refuses to rule out Scotland interest - gossip
item.description: Rangers linked with midfielders in Norway and Serbia and would former Liverpool and Real Madrid manager be interested in Scotland job?

item.title: Who has your Premiership club brought in & let go this summer?
item.description: Your one-stop shop for all the ins and outs in the Scottish Premiership during the 2026 summer transfer window.

item.title: Chelsea's Bartel closing in on move to Juventus
item.description: Chelsea midfielder Julia Bartel is closing in on a move to Italian club Juventus this summer.

item.title: Arsenal sign Germany striker Cerci on free
item.description: Arsenal have confirmed the signing of Germany striker Selina Cerci from Hoffenheim on a free transfer.

item.title: Coach was 'happy' to have Maddy Cusack on team
item.description: Jonathan Morgan, Maddy Cusack's coach at Sheffield United, gave evidence earlier on Tuesday.

item.title: Kirk named Durham boss two years after Leicester sacking
item.description: Willie Kirk has been appointed head coach of WSL 2 side Durham two years after he was sacked by Leicester City Women's team for having a relationship with a player.

item.title: Beever-Jones signs new contract until 2030 with Chelsea
item.description: England forward Aggie Beever-Jones signs a new contract with Chelsea, extending her stay at the club she joined as a nine-year-old until 2030.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.
